column1: The management is extremely chaotic. It’s so crowded that you can’t even see what’s inside the pit. At the same time, it’s far too commercialized, and the overall experience is really poor.

I walked quite a long way,  and maybe because I didn't join a guided tour, the experience felt pretty average.  I guess most people come here for its cultural and historical significance and to understand the meaning behind the ancient water-control project.  Personally,  though,  I didn't find the 80-yuan ticket very worthwhile.
